question.text: A bullish vertical spread using puts involves:

answer.text: Selling a higher strike put and buying a lower strike put
Buying a higher strike put and selling a lower strike put
Buying two calls at different strikes
Selling two puts at the same strike

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Bull put spread: Short put (higher strike, higher premium) + Long put (lower strike, lower premium). Net = premium received. Profit if USDINR stays above higher strike. **The Trap:** Bear put spread is the OPPOSITE — buy higher strike put, sell lower strike put. **Cross-Exam Context:** Put spreads are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Bull put spread is a NET premium RECEIPT strategy (you collect more than you pay). It profits from stable or rising prices.
